speaker: CRANMER
text: I hope I am not too late, and yet the gentleman

speaker: CRANMER
text: That was sent to me from the Council prayed me

speaker: CRANMER
text: To make great haste.

speaker: CRANMER
text: All fast? What means this? Ho!

speaker: CRANMER
text: Who waits there?

speaker: CRANMER
text: Sure you know me!

speaker: KEEPER
text: Yes, my lord,

speaker: KEEPER
text: But yet I cannot help you.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Why?

speaker: KEEPER
text: Your Grace must wait till you be called for.

speaker: CRANMER
text: So.

speaker: BUTTS
text: This is a piece of malice. I am glad

speaker: BUTTS
text: I came this way so happily. The King

speaker: BUTTS
text: Shall understand it presently.

speaker: CRANMER
text: ’Tis Butts,

speaker: CRANMER
text: The King’s physician. As he passed along

speaker: CRANMER
text: How earnestly he cast his eyes upon me!

speaker: CRANMER
text: Pray heaven he sound not my disgrace. For certain

speaker: CRANMER
text: This is of purpose laid by some that hate me—

speaker: CRANMER
text: God turn their hearts! I never sought their malice—

speaker: CRANMER
text: To quench mine honor. They would shame to make me

speaker: CRANMER
text: Wait else at door, a fellow councillor,

speaker: CRANMER
text: ’Mong boys, grooms, and lackeys. But their pleasures

speaker: CRANMER
text: Must be fulfilled, and I attend with patience.

speaker: BUTTS
text: I’ll show your Grace the strangest sight.

speaker: KING
text: What’s that, Butts?

speaker: BUTTS
text: I think your Highness saw this many a day.

speaker: KING
text: Body o’ me, where is it?

speaker: BUTTS
text: There, my lord:

speaker: BUTTS
text: The high promotion of his Grace of Canterbury,

speaker: BUTTS
text: Who holds his state at door, ’mongst pursuivants,

speaker: BUTTS
text: Pages, and footboys.

speaker: KING
text: Ha! ’Tis he indeed.

speaker: KING
text: Is this the honor they do one another?

speaker: KING
text: ’Tis well there’s one above ’em yet. I had thought

speaker: KING
text: They had parted so much honesty among ’em—

speaker: KING
text: At least good manners—as not thus to suffer

speaker: KING
text: A man of his place, and so near our favor,

speaker: KING
text: To dance attendance on their Lordships’ pleasures,

speaker: KING
text: And at the door, too, like a post with packets.

speaker: KING
text: By holy Mary, Butts, there’s knavery!

speaker: KING
text: Let ’em alone, and draw the curtain close.

speaker: KING
text: We shall hear more anon.

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Speak to the business, Master Secretary.

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Why are we met in council?

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Please your honors,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: The chief cause concerns his Grace of Canterbury.

speaker: GARDINER
text: Has he had knowledge of it?

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Yes.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Who waits there?

speaker: KEEPER
text: Without, my noble lords?

speaker: GARDINER
text: Yes.

speaker: KEEPER
text: My lord Archbishop,

speaker: KEEPER
text: And has done half an hour, to know your pleasures.

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Let him come in.

speaker: KEEPER
text: Your Grace may enter now.

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: My good lord Archbishop, I’m very sorry

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: To sit here at this present and behold

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: That chair stand empty. But we all are men,

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: In our own natures frail, and capable

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Of our flesh—few are angels—out of which frailty

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: And want of wisdom you, that best should teach us,

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Have misdemeaned yourself, and not a little,

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Toward the King first, then his laws, in filling

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: The whole realm, by your teaching and your chaplains’—

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: For so we are informed—with new opinions,

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Divers and dangerous, which are heresies

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: And, not reformed, may prove pernicious.

speaker: GARDINER
text: Which reformation must be sudden too,

speaker: GARDINER
text: My noble lords; for those that tame wild horses

speaker: GARDINER
text: Pace ’em not in their hands to make ’em gentle,

speaker: GARDINER
text: But stop their mouths with stubborn bits, and spur ’em

speaker: GARDINER
text: Till they obey the manage. If we suffer,

speaker: GARDINER
text: Out of our easiness and childish pity

speaker: GARDINER
text: To one man’s honor, this contagious sickness,

speaker: GARDINER
text: Farewell, all physic. And what follows then?

speaker: GARDINER
text: Commotions, uproars, with a general taint

speaker: GARDINER
text: Of the whole state, as of late days our neighbors,

speaker: GARDINER
text: The upper Germany, can dearly witness,

speaker: GARDINER
text: Yet freshly pitied in our memories.

speaker: CRANMER
text: My good lords, hitherto, in all the progress

speaker: CRANMER
text: Both of my life and office, I have labored,

speaker: CRANMER
text: And with no little study, that my teaching

speaker: CRANMER
text: And the strong course of my authority

speaker: CRANMER
text: Might go one way and safely; and the end

speaker: CRANMER
text: Was ever to do well. Nor is there living—

speaker: CRANMER
text: I speak it with a single heart, my lords—

speaker: CRANMER
text: A man that more detests, more stirs against,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Both in his private conscience and his place,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Defacers of a public peace than I do.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Pray heaven the King may never find a heart

speaker: CRANMER
text: With less allegiance in it! Men that make

speaker: CRANMER
text: Envy and crookèd malice nourishment

speaker: CRANMER
text: Dare bite the best. I do beseech your Lordships

speaker: CRANMER
text: That, in this case of justice, my accusers,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Be what they will, may stand forth face to face

speaker: CRANMER
text: And freely urge against me.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Nay, my lord,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That cannot be. You are a councillor,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And by that virtue no man dare accuse you.

speaker: GARDINER
text: My lord, because we have business of more moment,

speaker: GARDINER
text: We will be short with you. ’Tis his Highness’ pleasure,

speaker: GARDINER
text: And our consent, for better trial of you

speaker: GARDINER
text: From hence you be committed to the Tower,

speaker: GARDINER
text: Where, being but a private man again,

speaker: GARDINER
text: You shall know many dare accuse you boldly—

speaker: GARDINER
text: More than, I fear, you are provided for.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Ah, my good Lord of Winchester, I thank you.

speaker: CRANMER
text: You are always my good friend. If your will pass,

speaker: CRANMER
text: I shall both find your Lordship judge and juror,

speaker: CRANMER
text: You are so merciful. I see your end:

speaker: CRANMER
text: ’Tis my undoing. Love and meekness, lord,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Become a churchman better than ambition.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Win straying souls with modesty again;

speaker: CRANMER
text: Cast none away. That I shall clear myself,

speaker: CRANMER
text: Lay all the weight you can upon my patience,

speaker: CRANMER
text: I make as little doubt as you do conscience

speaker: CRANMER
text: In doing daily wrongs. I could say more,

speaker: CRANMER
text: But reverence to your calling makes me modest.

speaker: GARDINER
text: My lord, my lord, you are a sectary.

speaker: GARDINER
text: That’s the plain truth. Your painted gloss discovers,

speaker: GARDINER
text: To men that understand you, words and weakness.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: My Lord of Winchester, you’re a little,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: By your good favor, too sharp. Men so noble,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: However faulty, yet should find respect

speaker: CROMWELL
text: For what they have been. ’Tis a cruelty

speaker: CROMWELL
text: To load a falling man.

speaker: GARDINER
text: Good Master Secretary—

speaker: GARDINER
text: I cry your Honor mercy—you may worst

speaker: GARDINER
text: Of all this table say so.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Why, my lord?

speaker: GARDINER
text: Do not I know you for a favorer

speaker: GARDINER
text: Of this new sect? You are not sound.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Not sound?

speaker: GARDINER
text: Not sound, I say.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Would you were half so honest!

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Men’s prayers then would seek you, not their fears.

speaker: GARDINER
text: I shall remember this bold language.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Do.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Remember your bold life too.

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: This is too much!

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Forbear, for shame, my lords.

speaker: GARDINER
text: I have done.

speaker: CROMWELL
text: And I.

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Then thus for you, my lord: it stands agreed,

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: I take it, by all voices, that forthwith

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: You be conveyed to th’ Tower a prisoner,

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: There to remain till the King’s further pleasure

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Be known unto us.—Are you all agreed, lords?

speaker: ALL
text: We are.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Is there no other way of mercy

speaker: CRANMER
text: But I must needs to th’ Tower, my lords?

speaker: GARDINER
text: What other

speaker: GARDINER
text: Would you expect? You are strangely troublesome.

speaker: GARDINER
text: Let some o’ th’ guard be ready there.

speaker: CRANMER
text: For me?

speaker: CRANMER
text: Must I go like a traitor thither?

speaker: GARDINER
text: Receive him,

speaker: GARDINER
text: And see him safe i’ th’ Tower.

speaker: CRANMER
text: Stay, good my lords,

speaker: CRANMER
text: I have a little yet to say. Look there, my lords.

speaker: CRANMER
text: By virtue of that ring, I take my cause

speaker: CRANMER
text: Out of the grips of cruel men and give it

speaker: CRANMER
text: To a most noble judge, the King my master.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: This is the King’s ring.

speaker: SURREY
text: ’Tis no counterfeit.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: ’Tis the right ring, by heaven! I told you all,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: When we first put this dangerous stone a-rolling,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: ’Twould fall upon ourselves.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Do you think, my lords,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: The King will suffer but the little finger

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Of this man to be vexed?

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: ’Tis now too certain.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: How much more is his life in value with him!

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Would I were fairly out on ’t!

speaker: CROMWELL
text: My mind gave me,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: In seeking tales and informations

speaker: CROMWELL
text: Against this man, whose honesty the devil

speaker: CROMWELL
text: And his disciples only envy at,

speaker: CROMWELL
text: You blew the fire that burns you. Now, have at you!

speaker: GARDINER
text: Dread sovereign, how much are we bound to heaven

speaker: GARDINER
text: In daily thanks, that gave us such a prince,

speaker: GARDINER
text: Not only good and wise, but most religious;

speaker: GARDINER
text: One that in all obedience makes the Church

speaker: GARDINER
text: The chief aim of his honor, and to strengthen

speaker: GARDINER
text: That holy duty out of dear respect,

speaker: GARDINER
text: His royal self in judgment comes to hear

speaker: GARDINER
text: The cause betwixt her and this great offender.

speaker: KING
text: You were ever good at sudden commendations,

speaker: KING
text: Bishop of Winchester. But know I come not

speaker: KING
text: To hear such flattery now, and in my presence

speaker: KING
text: They are too thin and base to hide offenses.

speaker: KING
text: To me you cannot reach. You play the spaniel,

speaker: KING
text: And think with wagging of your tongue to win me;

speaker: KING
text: But whatsoe’er thou tak’st me for, I’m sure

speaker: KING
text: Thou hast a cruel nature and a bloody.—

speaker: KING
text: Good man, sit down.

speaker: KING
text: Now let me see the proudest

speaker: KING
text: He, that dares most, but wag his finger at thee.

speaker: KING
text: By all that’s holy, he had better starve

speaker: KING
text: Than but once think this place becomes thee not.

speaker: SURREY
text: May it please your Grace—

speaker: KING
text: No, sir, it does not please me.

speaker: KING
text: I had thought I had had men of some understanding

speaker: KING
text: And wisdom of my Council, but I find none.

speaker: KING
text: Was it discretion, lords, to let this man,

speaker: KING
text: This good man—few of you deserve that title—

speaker: KING
text: This honest man, wait like a lousy footboy

speaker: KING
text: At chamber door? And one as great as you are?

speaker: KING
text: Why, what a shame was this! Did my commission

speaker: KING
text: Bid you so far forget yourselves? I gave you

speaker: KING
text: Power as he was a councillor to try him,

speaker: KING
text: Not as a groom. There’s some of you, I see,

speaker: KING
text: More out of malice than integrity,

speaker: KING
text: Would try him to the utmost, had you mean,

speaker: KING
text: Which you shall never have while I live.

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Thus far,

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: My most dread sovereign, may it like your Grace

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: To let my tongue excuse all. What was purposed

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: Concerning his imprisonment was rather,

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: If there be faith in men, meant for his trial

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: And fair purgation to the world than malice,

speaker: CHANCELLOR
text: I’m sure, in me.

speaker: KING
text: Well, well, my lords, respect him.

speaker: KING
text: Take him, and use him well; he’s worthy of it.

speaker: KING
text: I will say thus much for him: if a prince

speaker: KING
text: May be beholding to a subject, I

speaker: KING
text: Am, for his love and service, so to him.

speaker: KING
text: Make me no more ado, but all embrace him.

speaker: KING
text: Be friends, for shame, my lords.

speaker: KING
text: My Lord of Canterbury,

speaker: KING
text: I have a suit which you must not deny me:

speaker: KING
text: That is, a fair young maid that yet wants baptism.

speaker: KING
text: You must be godfather and answer for her.

speaker: CRANMER
text: The greatest monarch now alive may glory

speaker: CRANMER
text: In such an honor. How may I deserve it,

speaker: CRANMER
text: That am a poor and humble subject to you?

speaker: KING
text: Come, come, my lord, you’d spare your spoons.

speaker: KING
text: You shall have two noble partners with you: the

speaker: KING
text: old Duchess of Norfolk and Lady Marquess Dorset.

speaker: KING
text: Will these please you?—

speaker: KING
text: Once more, my lord of Winchester, I charge you,

speaker: KING
text: Embrace and love this man.

speaker: GARDINER
text: With a true heart

speaker: GARDINER
text: And brother-love I do it.

speaker: CRANMER
text: And let heaven

speaker: CRANMER
text: Witness how dear I hold this confirmation.

speaker: KING
text: Good man, those joyful tears show thy true heart.

speaker: KING
text: The common voice, I see, is verified

speaker: KING
text: Of thee, which says thus: “Do my Lord of Canterbury

speaker: KING
text: A shrewd turn, and he’s your friend forever.”—

speaker: KING
text: Come, lords, we trifle time away. I long

speaker: KING
text: To have this young one made a Christian.

speaker: KING
text: As I have made you one, lords, one remain.

speaker: KING
text: So I grow stronger, you more honor gain.
